mechanism_narrative: Persistently low unemployment (UNRATE) signals a tight labor market with rising wages and consumer spending, which supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns. The signal strength is proportional to how far UNRATE is below its natural rate.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC for UNRATE over 20-day forward returns is above -0.05 (i.e., not negative and significant), or the predictive signal does not beat a simple baseline (e.g., mean return) in a t-test at 5% significance over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, it enters historically restrictive territory that tightens financial conditions, increases discount rates, and depresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and the subsequent 60-day S&P 500 return is positive in more than 40% of instances, or the average return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05), the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) and industrial production (INDPRO) indicates robust demand and supply synchronization, which signals strong economic momentum that boosts corporate profits and equity prices over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If in months with both proxies rising above thresholds, the subsequent 20-day S&P 500 return is not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) or the signal does not achieve a Sharpe ratio above 0.3 over 5-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a full-employment regime (UNRATE below 5%), rising payrolls (PAYEMS) strongly amplify wage growth and consumer spending, making the positive impact on equity returns more pronounced than when slack exists. The signal relies on the interaction between the proxy and the regime variable.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE < 5% and PAYEMS increasing over prior month
failure_condition: If when UNRATE < 5% and PAYEMS increases, the 20-day forward return is not significantly higher than when UNRATE >= 5% (difference in means test p > 0.05), or if the signal fails to produce positive returns in at least 60% of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which signals higher future corporate earnings and supports bullish equity market conditions over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC for INDPRO over 20-day forward returns is below 0.04, or the top quartile of INDPRO changes does not produce significantly higher returns than the bottom quartile (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in retail sales (RSAFS) exceeding 2% signals an abrupt demand contraction that triggers downward earnings revisions and investor panic, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If after a >2% m/m decline in RSAFS the 5-day S&P 500 return is positive in more than 30% of cases, or the average return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10), the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of rising unemployment (UNRATE increasing) and falling federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS decreasing) signals a coordinated policy easing response to economic weakness, which historically reduces recession fears and boosts equity prices over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS down over 3 months and UNRATE up over 3 months
regime_hint: easing cycle response to labor weakness
failure_condition: If in periods with both conditions met the 60-day forward return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or the average return is below the unconditional mean, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: During high inflation regimes (CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth above 3%), increases in the federal funds rate act as aggressive tightening that raises recession risk and compresses equity valuations, leading to negative 20-day forward returns. The regime condition changes the sign of the rate-return relationship.
failure_condition: If when CPI YoY > 3% and FEDFUNDS rises, the 20-day S&P 500 return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) or the signal produces positive returns in more than 40% of instances, the hypothesis is falsified.
